items.title: Image classification with random flips and crops
items.scenario: A 5,000-image dog/cat dataset overfits: 98% train accuracy, 72% validation. Training adds random horizontal flips and random resized crops (224×224) each epoch without new labels.
items.explanation: The CNN must recognize dogs and cats under framing and mirror variation — variance drops. Validation accuracy rises to ~84% while train accuracy moderates to ~92%, a healthier gap.

items.title: Text sentiment with synonym replacement
items.scenario: Product reviews with 800 labeled rows; a bag-of-words model hits 95% train F1 but 68% validation F1. Training replaces 10% of non-stopword tokens with WordNet synonyms when polarity is unchanged.
items.explanation: Surface form varies while labels stay fixed — the model relies less on exact word co-occurrences from the small training set. Validation F1 improves if replacements do not flip sentiment.

items.title: Tabular regression with Gaussian feature noise
items.scenario: House price prediction with 600 rows; a deep MLP shows low train MSE and high val MSE. Each training step adds zero-mean noise (σ = 2% of column std) to numeric inputs.
items.explanation: Noise simulates measurement error and discourages fitting exact training coordinates — a mild regularizer. Val MSE often improves slightly; too much noise can underfit.

items.title: SMOTE for imbalanced fraud detection
items.scenario: Fraud labels are 0.5% of 50k transactions; a classifier predicts "not fraud" for almost everything with high train accuracy. SMOTE oversamples the minority class in feature space before training.
items.explanation: Synthetic minority points reduce majority-head memorization. This is augmentation-style oversampling — not deep SMOTE theory here — paired with proper validation that does not leak synthetic rows into test.

items.title: Augmentation that breaks the task
items.scenario: Digit recognition on MNIST with random 90° rotation enabled; many 6s become 9s and 9s become 6s after rotation.
items.explanation: Rotation is not label-preserving for upright digits — the model learns wrong invariances and validation accuracy falls. Fix by restricting rotation range or disabling it for this task.
